forces and newton's laws.prompt: A car is moving at a constant velocity. What can be said about the resultant force acting on it?
quizOptions: The resultant force is zero.
The resultant force is in the direction of motion.
The resultant force is opposite to the direction of motion.
The resultant force is increasing.

markScheme.point: The resultant force acting on the car is zero.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: When an object moves at a constant velocity, the resultant force acting on it is zero.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Explain how the normal contact force acts on an object placed on a horizontal surface
quizOptions: The normal contact force acts perpendicular to the surface.
The normal contact force acts in the direction of motion.
The normal contact force acts opposite to the direction of motion.
The normal contact force acts at an angle to the surface.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: The normal contact force acts perpendicular to the surface.

markScheme.point: The normal contact force acts perpendicular to the surface.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: The normal contact force acts perpendicular to the surface, supporting the object.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Define the term 'resultant force' and state its effect on an object's motion
quizOptions: Resultant force is the vector sum of forces and causes acceleration.
Resultant force is the scalar sum of forces and causes deceleration.
Resultant force is the vector sum of forces and has no effect on motion.
Resultant force is the scalar sum of forces and causes acceleration.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Resultant force is the vector sum of forces and causes acceleration.

markScheme.point: The resultant force is the vector sum of all forces acting on an object.
The resultant force causes acceleration in its direction.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: The resultant force is the vector sum of forces acting on an object and causes acceleration in its direction.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: State the condition under which an object will have zero acceleration
quizOptions: When the resultant force is zero.
When the force of gravity is zero.
When the normal contact force is zero.
When the frictional force is zero.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: When the resultant force is zero.

markScheme.point: An object will have zero acceleration when the resultant force acting on it is zero.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: An object has zero acceleration when the resultant force acting on it is zero.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Compare the direction of the normal contact force and the force of gravity acting on an object on a horizontal surface
quizOptions: The normal contact force acts upward and the force of gravity acts downward.
The normal contact force acts downward and the force of gravity acts upward.
Both forces act in the same direction.
Both forces act at an angle to the surface.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: The normal contact force acts upward and the force of gravity acts downward.

markScheme.point: The normal contact force acts upward, perpendicular to the surface.
The force of gravity acts downward, toward the center of the Earth.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: On a horizontal surface, the normal contact force acts upward and the force of gravity acts downward.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: State the effect of air resistance on an object moving through the air
quizOptions: Air resistance opposes the motion of the object.
Air resistance aids the motion of the object.
Air resistance has no effect on the motion of the object.
Air resistance increases the motion of the object.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Air resistance opposes the motion of the object.

markScheme.point: Air resistance opposes the motion of an object moving through the air.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: Air resistance is a force that opposes the motion of an object as it moves through the air.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Explain how friction acts between two surfaces in contact
quizOptions: Friction opposes relative motion between the surfaces.
Friction aids relative motion between the surfaces.
Friction has no effect on relative motion between the surfaces.
Friction increases relative motion between the surfaces.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Friction opposes relative motion between the surfaces.

markScheme.point: Friction acts between surfaces in contact and opposes relative motion or the tendency of relative motion.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: Friction is a force that opposes relative motion between two surfaces in contact.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: State the effect of a resultant force on an object's motion
quizOptions: The resultant force causes acceleration in its direction.
The resultant force causes deceleration in its direction.
The resultant force has no effect on the motion.
The resultant force causes motion in the opposite direction.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: The resultant force causes acceleration in its direction.

markScheme.point: The resultant force causes acceleration in its direction.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: The resultant force acting on an object causes acceleration in its direction, as described by Newton's Second Law of Motion.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Explain how the direction of the force of gravity affects the motion of a projectile
quizOptions: Gravity affects the horizontal motion of the projectile.
Gravity causes vertical acceleration of the projectile.
Gravity has no effect on the motion of the projectile.
Gravity causes the projectile to move in a straight line.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Gravity causes vertical acceleration of the projectile.

markScheme.point: The force of gravity acts vertically downwards on the projectile.
The force of gravity causes a vertical acceleration of the projectile.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: The force of gravity acts vertically downwards and causes a vertical acceleration of the projectile.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: State the relationship between the mass of an object and its acceleration when a constant force is applied
quizOptions: The acceleration is directly proportional to the mass.
The acceleration is inversely proportional to the mass.
The mass has no effect on the acceleration.
The acceleration is directly proportional to the square of the mass.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: The acceleration is inversely proportional to the mass.

markScheme.point: For a constant force, the acceleration of an object is inversely proportional to its mass.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: According to Newton's second law, F = ma, so for a constant force, a is inversely proportional to m.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Compare the effect of air resistance on a skydiver before and after opening their parachute
quizOptions: Air resistance is the same before and after opening the parachute.
Air resistance is greater before opening the parachute.
Air resistance opposes motion and is greater after opening the parachute.
Air resistance has no effect on the skydiver's motion.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Air resistance opposes motion and is greater after opening the parachute.

markScheme.point: Air resistance opposes the motion of the skydiver.
The air resistance is greater after the parachute is opened, due to the increased surface area.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: Air resistance opposes the motion of the skydiver and is greater after the parachute is opened.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Explain how friction acts between two surfaces in contact when they are moving relative to each other
quizOptions: Friction acts perpendicular to the surfaces and causes the objects to stick together.
Friction acts parallel to the surfaces and opposes their relative motion.
Friction has no effect on the motion of the objects.
Friction causes the objects to move faster.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Friction acts parallel to the surfaces and opposes their relative motion.

markScheme.point: Friction acts between the two surfaces in contact and opposes their relative motion.
The direction of the frictional force is parallel to the surfaces in contact.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: Friction acts between the two surfaces in contact, opposing their relative motion, and its direction is parallel to the surfaces.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Explain how the force of gravity affects the motion of an object on an inclined plane
quizOptions: Gravity has no effect on the motion of the object.
Gravity causes the object to move up the plane.
Gravity causes the object to move down the plane.
Gravity causes the object to move perpendicular to the plane.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: Gravity causes the object to move down the plane.

markScheme.point: The force of gravity acts vertically downwards on the object.
The component of gravity parallel to the inclined plane causes the object to accelerate down the plane.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: The force of gravity acts vertically downwards and has a component parallel to the inclined plane, causing the object to accelerate down the plane.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: State the condition under which an object will have a constant acceleration
quizOptions: When the net force acting on the object is zero.
When the net force acting on the object is constant.
When the mass of the object is constant.
When the velocity of the object is constant.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: When the net force acting on the object is constant.

markScheme.point: An object will have a constant acceleration when a constant resultant force acts on it.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: According to Newton's second law, a constant resultant force causes a constant acceleration.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: A car is traveling at a constant velocity. What can be said about the resultant force acting on the car?
quizOptions: The resultant force is zero.
The resultant force is non-zero.
The resultant force is acting in the opposite direction to the car's motion.
The resultant force is acting in the same direction as the car's motion.

markScheme.point: The resultant force acting on the car is zero.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: When an object is traveling at a constant velocity, the resultant force acting on it is zero.

quizOptions: The vector sum of all forces acting on an object.
The largest force acting on an object.
The smallest force acting on an object.
A single force acting on an object.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: The vector sum of all forces acting on an object.

markScheme.point: The resultant force is the vector sum of all forces acting on an object.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: The resultant force is the vector sum of all forces acting on an object, and it determines the object's motion.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: Compare the direction of the resultant force with the direction of acceleration
quizOptions: The resultant force and acceleration are in the same direction.
The resultant force and acceleration are in opposite directions.
The resultant force is zero, but acceleration is non-zero.
The resultant force is non-zero, but acceleration is zero.
forces and newton's laws.quizCorrectAnswer: The resultant force and acceleration are in the same direction.

markScheme.point: The resultant force and acceleration are in the same direction.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: The direction of the resultant force is the same as the direction of acceleration, as stated by Newton's second law.

forces and newton's laws.prompt: State the condition under which an object will have zero acceleration
quizOptions: The resultant force is zero.
The resultant force is non-zero.
The object is at rest.
The object is moving with a constant velocity.

markScheme.point: An object will have zero acceleration when the resultant force acting on it is zero.

forces and newton's laws.workedSolution: According to Newton's first law, an object will have zero acceleration when the resultant force acting on it is zero.

projectile motion.prompt: Which component of velocity remains constant in ideal projectile motion when air resistance is ignored?
quizOptions: Horizontal velocity
Vertical velocity
Resultant velocity
Terminal velocity

markScheme.point: Identifies the horizontal component of velocity as the component that remains constant.

projectile motion.workedSolution: When air resistance is ignored, there is no horizontal resultant force, so the horizontal acceleration is zero and the horizontal velocity remains constant.

projectile motion.prompt: Explain the acceleration of a projectile near Earth's surface when air resistance is ignored.
quizOptions: 9.8 m/s^2 downward
9.8 m/s^2 upward
It changes with speed
shortAcceptedAnswers: constant 9.8 metres per second squared downward

markScheme.point: States the acceleration magnitude as 9.8 m/s^2 or g, including a valid acceleration unit or the symbol g.
States that the acceleration is downward or towards Earth.

projectile motion.workedSolution: The projectile has a constant acceleration of approximately 9.8 m/s^2 vertically downward because gravity is the only force acting on it.

projectile motion.prompt: At the highest point of a projectile's path, what is its vertical velocity?
quizOptions: Zero
Maximum
Equal to horizontal velocity
Equal to acceleration

markScheme.point: States that the vertical velocity is zero at the highest point.

projectile motion.workedSolution: At the highest point, the projectile stops moving upward momentarily, so its vertical velocity is zero.

projectile motion.prompt: What shape is the path of an ideal projectile?
quizOptions: Parabola
Circle
Ellipse
Straight line

markScheme.point: States that the path of an ideal projectile is parabolic.

projectile motion.workedSolution: The horizontal motion is uniform while the vertical motion has constant acceleration, producing a parabolic path.

projectile motion.prompt: Which force acts on an ideal projectile after launch when air resistance is ignored?
quizOptions: Weight
Thrust
Drag
Normal reaction

markScheme.point: States that weight, gravity, or the gravitational force acts on the projectile after launch.

projectile motion.workedSolution: After launch, the only force acting on an ideal projectile is its weight due to gravity.

projectile motion.prompt: For launch and landing at the same height, which launch angle gives maximum range?
quizOptions: 45 degrees
30 degrees
60 degrees
90 degrees

markScheme.point: States that a launch angle of 45 degrees gives the maximum range when launch and landing heights are equal.

projectile motion.workedSolution: For a fixed launch speed and equal launch and landing heights, the maximum horizontal range occurs at 45 degrees.

projectile motion.prompt: If initial speed is fixed, what happens to time of flight when the vertical launch component increases?
quizOptions: It increases
It decreases
It becomes zero
It is unaffected

markScheme.point: States that the time of flight increases when the vertical launch component increases.

projectile motion.workedSolution: A larger vertical launch component means the projectile takes longer to rise and return, so the total time of flight increases.

projectile motion.prompt: Which initial velocity component determines the range when time of flight is known?
quizOptions: Horizontal component
Vertical component
Acceleration component
Resultant component

markScheme.point: Identifies the horizontal velocity component as determining the range when time of flight is known.

projectile motion.workedSolution: The horizontal range equals the horizontal velocity multiplied by the time of flight.

projectile motion.prompt: What assumption allows horizontal acceleration to be zero in projectile motion?
quizOptions: No air resistance
Constant mass
Small launch angle
High launch speed

markScheme.point: States that air resistance is ignored or that there is no drag.

projectile motion.workedSolution: When air resistance is ignored, there is no horizontal force acting on the projectile, so its horizontal acceleration is zero.

projectile motion.prompt: What is the horizontal displacement of a projectile commonly called?
quizOptions: Range
Height
Amplitude
Period

markScheme.point: States that the horizontal displacement of a projectile is called its range.

projectile motion.workedSolution: The horizontal distance travelled by a projectile from launch to landing is called its range.

projectile motion.prompt: Explain why the horizontal velocity of a projectile remains constant when air resistance is ignored.
quizOptions: There is no horizontal resultant force
Gravity acts horizontally
The vertical velocity is zero
The projectile has no weight
projectile motion.quizCorrectAnswer: There is no horizontal resultant force

markScheme.point: States that there is no horizontal resultant force acting on the projectile.
Explicitly links the lack of horizontal resultant force to zero horizontal acceleration or to the horizontal velocity remaining constant.

projectile motion.workedSolution: With no air resistance, no horizontal resultant force acts on the projectile. Therefore, the horizontal acceleration is zero and the horizontal velocity remains constant.
